reviews.review_description: It cool but very short if there was a update it can get better

Ever since my ketamine dealer died I have been lacking a reason to get up and feed the child in my basement ketamine WAS my life support the only thing the kept me thriving and the only thing the made me, me! Ever since two tooth Joe broke the news to me that my dealer had died in a gunfight with the police I haven’t felt like me I needed an inspiration and it had been days since I  checked on the child in my basement. News recently broke to me that the fbi had tracked my location and found out I’m the notorious child kidnapper I have been on edge so I found a Vr in this house that I had just wondered my way into and I decided to put it on I looked for the games like Bonelab and blade and sorcery but I hadn’t found a hint until I realized only one game rest in my presence it was the game that goes by the name of Just Up and I started playing it to my surprise the radiant Oppenheimer lay in my sight and it pushed a button into my brain that instantly evaporated my endless depression and the next thing I knew I was both the strongest man alive and weaker than Stephen Hawking in a fist fight against Mike Tyson in his prime.. But that didn’t distract me as I had seen a chest that could only obtain my wildest of dreams but the key lay only a few feet away taunting my presence as it must’ve weighed the weight of the heaviest star in the universe.. It wouldn’t even budge I instantly started training myself to pick up the key I trained making leaps across the famous leap of faith I felt the adrenaline pumping through my veins as my arms shrieked at immense pain that’s when I realized the ominous tower of raw ground beef standing in my presence… I was almost ready to lift the galactic key until a swat team raided the house I heard windows shattering all around me but that didn’t stop me from jumping back and fourth risking my life as bullets pierced my arms only to slow me but I wasn’t fazed I was determined to lift that key…. So I kept fighting I had almost been drained of my blood at this point but not even the most powerful being could intervene in my attempt at achieving my goals. Until I found myself on the ground crawling to the key I had maybe and ounce of blood left but I.. I had to lift it even if it costed my life but it didn’t even budge.. to this day I’m still recovering from the attacks of corrupt law enforcement but someday mark my words I WILL RETURN!

Ever since the FBI discovered my underground cult of drugging innocent civilians found along the street and selling their organs for money, my life has simply gone downhill. My family doesn’t accept me, my lover doesn’t accept me, not even the local priest says I can be forgiven. I have gone anonymous  in this world, and ever since this occurrence, I’ve been living in a absolute rancidity of a cabin in northeastern Nunavut. The way I eat is by hunting bear with a knife I made from a rusty sheet of metal and some wood found in the forest next to my cabin. My life has been very remote, that is until I storm my oculus from the local Best Buy last year. I’ve met many kind and caring people and have been enjoying myself since. Today, i was scrolling on social media apps on my jailbroken phone, and saw a streamer who goes by “Kai Cenat” playing “Only Up”. I was very confused with this and was interested in playing this game. But the things is, I have no PC. So I did the next bed thing, and went to my oculus. And that, is where I found this work of art. I looked at the images displayed for this game, and I was very interested, so I got it. Although the game is 200 megabytes, my router is an Apple core with parts of clothing hangers to produce “internet”. Because my router runs 4 bytes at its best, it took roughly three decades for it to download. When I got the notification, I immediately sprinted up from my olive green rotten chair I got from the junkyard 5 miles away, lit the four candles I had so I wouldn’t lose tracking, and put on the headset. I clicked on Just Up, and that is when the glorious facial structure of Oppenheimer came to its glory in front of me. I was in awe, ecstatic about what was about to come. I loaded in expecting the supposed only up to appear, but instead I got something 500 times better. I have a spinal disfunction that causes me to not have the ability to look sideways. Immediately I saw a blocky chest in my sight, with a key behind that couldn’t be picked up(must not be worthy enough to touch the masterpiece), and I also noticed that I was standing on a studded roblox platform. I turned to the left and that when’s I saw what I like to refer to as “the jump of glory”, mouth agape, I walked towards it and kept jumping forwards and backwards without a thought of my disgusting past. This is the most happy I’ve been in 49 years. While I was jumping, I spotted something in my peripheral. It was big, red, and in the distance, I turned to see what it was, and that’s when my eyes laid upon it. The meaty monument. I couldn’t stop staring at it. It was beautiful. Magnificent. Exquisite. Delightful. I had tears of joy. I cried so loud you could hear it from Santiago. I truly believe that this game might just be the most appealing item on the planet. It was an honor to play this game, and I hope can play it even more in the forsaken future. I was diagnosed with an incurable disease caused by my ketamine addiction, and am expected to die in six days. I usually can’t get up, but now when this game is in my mind, I can jolt with joy. I give my thanks to you, Just Up.
_____________________________________________
How this game correlates to LeBron James and his career of a king.

LeBron James, often hailed as one of the greatest basketball players of all time, has had a remarkable career that spans over two decades. Born on December 30, 1984, in Akron, Ohio, LeBron quickly gained attention for his exceptional talent on the basketball court. He rose through the ranks of high school basketball, showcasing his skills at St. Vincent-St. Mary High School, where he led his team to a state championship and earned national acclaim. His extraordinary performance led to him being the first overall pick in the 2003 NBA Draft, selected by the Cleveland Cavaliers. Upon entering the NBA, LeBron made an immediate impact. He won the NBA Rookie of the Year award in 2004, averaging 20.9 points, 5.5 rebounds, and 5.9 assists per game. His combination of size, athleticism, and basketball IQ was unprecedented, and he quickly became a fan favorite. Despite his individual success, the Cavaliers struggled to become a championship contender. LeBron’s early years in Cleveland were marked by personal achievements but also frustration, as the team was unable to secure an NBA title during his first stint.
In 2010, after seven seasons with the Cavaliers, LeBron made a controversial decision to join the Miami Heat, forming a "superteam" with Dwyane Wade and Chris Bosh. This move sparked intense debate among fans and analysts alike. However, it proved to be a pivotal moment in his career. During his four seasons in Miami, LeBron reached the NBA Finals each year, winning two championships in 2012 and 2013. He also earned two NBA Finals MVP awards, further solidifying his legacy as a clutch performer in high-pressure situations. After his successful tenure in Miami, LeBron returned to the Cavaliers in 2014 with the goal of bringing a championship to his home state. In 2016, he accomplished this feat in dramatic fashion, leading the Cavaliers to a historic comeback against the Golden State Warriors in the NBA Finals. This victory was not just significant for LeBron personally but also for the city of Cleveland, which had not won a championship in over 50 years. LeBron’s leadership and performance during this series, including a memorable block in Game 7, are often cited as defining moments of his career. LeBron’s career continued to evolve as he transitioned to the Los Angeles Lakers in 2018. His impact in Los Angeles was immediate, both on and off the court. In 2020, he led the Lakers to an NBA championship, furthering his legacy and bringing the franchise its 17th title, which tied them with the Boston Celtics for the most in NBA history. LeBron's ability to adapt his game and lead different teams to success speaks volumes about his versatility and enduring talent. Beyond his on-court achievements, LeBron has also made a significant impact off the court. He is known for his philanthropy, particularly through the LeBron James Family Foundation, which focuses on education and community support. His I PROMISE School in Akron represents his commitment to giving back and providing opportunities for underprivileged children. LeBron's influence extends beyond basketball, as he uses his platform to address social issues, advocating for equality and justice. In summary, LeBron James’s career is a testament to his extraordinary talent, resilience, and impact both in sports and society. From his early days in Cleveland to his championships with Miami and Los Angeles, LeBron has consistently pushed the boundaries of excellence in basketball. As he continues to play and influence future generations, his legacy as one of the greatest players of all time is firmly established, making him an iconic figure in the history of sports.  Now how does this correlate? Well I’ll hand it to you in only one word. Eminence. Now what does this mean? Well to put it clearly, LeBron James is obviously the best basketball player of all time, dominating the league for two decades. And Just Up? Well not even two years. But even though it hasn’t even been that long, Just Up is putting up prime LeBron numbers. Portraying ownership of the meta quest store, this game should seriously be emitting a white light off of itself. I mean come on, this game is worshipped more than Kim Jong Un in North Korea. So to put it simply, LeBron and Just Up both portray greatness and dominance over their categories. Now this, is absolute cinema.

I expect to have what's in the pics and video but when I spawn in I see a beautiful Oppenheimer and then I become a spooky scary skeleton on a platform with a chest and a key that you can do nothing with aswell as my one and only friend Patrick the platform to my left
